In some cases, you may find courses which have more than one catalogue number assigned to a single meet (a single lecture, tutorial, lab etc.). Multiple catalogue numbers indicate that the course is cross-listed. For more information about the cross-listing, please see the course description in the Courses Web site.

AP SOSC F Health: International and Comparative Perspectives
  3103  3.00 A  EN ONLN  01  B73P01 
F11:30170Keele  
  
AP SOSC W Selected Topics in Health and Society
  3115  3.00 M  EN SEMR  01  E92T01 
T11:30170KeeleACW 003
 This course examines the intersectional impacts of Social Determinants of Health from infancy to adolescence. Students explore how geography, poverty, food security, war, trauma, sexuality, gender, mental health, homelessness, divorce, school, disability and media play an integral role in the lived experiences of children and their health outcomes as adults.

This file was last updated on Sat, 2 May 2026 at 03:32:51 AM.

In some cases, you may find courses which have more than one catalogue number assigned to a single meet (a single lecture, tutorial, lab etc.). Multiple catalogue numbers indicate that the course is cross-listed. For more information about the cross-listing, please see the course description in the Courses Web site.
